title: Linked orthopedic implant
abstract: Described herein is a linked orthopedic implant for an anatomical ball and socket joint comprising: a ball replacement linkage configured for attachment to a long bone of the ball and socket joint; a socket replacement linkage configured for attachment to a socket bone of the ball and socket joint; a universal joint coupled to the ball replacement linkage by a first hinge connector and coupled to the socket replacement linkage by a second hinge connector; the rotation axis of the first hinge connector offset from intersection with the rotation axis of the second hinge connector.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A linked orthopedic implant for an anatomical ball and socket joint comprising: a ball replacement linkage configured for attachment to a long bone of the ball and socket joint; a socket replacement linkage configured for attachment to a socket bone of the ball and socket joint; a universal joint coupled to the ball replacement linkage by a first hinge connector and coupled to the socket replacement linkage by a second hinge connector; a first axis of rotation defined by the first hinge connector offset from intersection with a second axis of rotation defined by the second hinge connector, the offset being an orthogonal distance of separation between the first axis of rotation and the second axis of rotation that is greater than the sum of a first radius of the first hinge connector and a second radius of the second hinge connector. 2. The implant of claim 1 , wherein the first and second hinge connectors are mounted on a central block. 3. The implant of claim 2, wherein the central block comprises a bend in between the first and second hinge connectors. 4. The implant of claim 3, wherein the bend has a bend angle measured as deviation of co- linearity of a line of symmetry of a portion of the central block housing the first hinge connector with a line of symmetry of a portion of the central block housing the second hinge connector, the bend angle being greater than 4 degrees and less than 80 degrees. 5. The implant of claim 2, wherein the first hinge connector comprises a first hinge pin having a central portion journaled to the central block and opposing end portions extending from first and second opposing sides of the central block, and the second hinge connector comprises a second hinge pin having a central portion journaled to the central block and opposing end portions extending from third and fourth opposing sides of the central block. 6. The implant of claim 1, wherein the ball replacement linkage comprises a first hinge attachment portion and a long bone attachment portion, and the first hinge attachment portion is coupled to the long bone attachment portion by a first axial joint. 7. The implant of claim 1, wherein the ball replacement linkage comprises a longitudinal shaft coupled to a first yoke, the first yoke coupled to the first hinge connector. 8. The implant of claim 1, wherein the socket replacement linkage comprises a second hinge attachment portion and a socket bone attachment portion, and the second hinge attachment portion is coupled to the socket bone attachment portion by a second axial joint. 9. The implant of claim 1 , wherein the socket replacement linkage comprises a socket mounting plate coupled to a second yoke, the second yoke coupled to the second hinge connector. 10. The implant of claim 9, wherein the second yoke maintains a fixed angled position relative to a central axis of the socket mounting plate, the fixed angle measured as a deviation from co linearity with the central axis, the fixed angle being greater than 4 degrees and less than 45 degrees. 11. A linked orthopedic implant for an anatomical ball and socket joint comprising: a ball replacement linkage configured for attachment to a long bone of the ball and socket joint; a socket replacement linkage configured for attachment to a socket bone of the ball and socket joint; a universal joint coupled to the ball replacement linkage by a first hinge connector and coupled to the socket replacement linkage by a second hinge connector; the rotation axis of the first hinge connector offset from intersection with the rotation axis of the second hinge connector. 12. The implant of claim 11, wherein the first and second hinge connectors are mounted on a central block. 13. The implant of claim 12, wherein the central block comprises a bend in between the first and second hinge connectors. 14. The implant of claim 13, wherein the bend has an angle of less than 40 degrees. 15. The implant of claim 12, wherein the first hinge connector comprises a first hinge pin having a central portion journaled to the central block and opposing end portions extending from first and second opposing sides of the central block, and the second hinge connector comprises a second hinge pin having a central portion journaled to the central block and opposing end portions extending from third and fourth opposing sides of the central block. 16. The implant of claim 1 1, wherein the ball replacement linkage comprises a first hinge attachment portion and a long bone attachment portion. 17. The implant of claim 16, wherein the first hinge attachment portion is coupled to the long bone attachment portion by a first axial joint. 18. The implant of claim 1 1, wherein the ball replacement linkage comprises a longitudinal shaft coupled to a first yoke, the first yoke coupled to the first hinge connector. 19. The implant of claim 18, wherein the longitudinal shaft is shaped for press fit in the intramedullary canal of the long bone. 20. The implant of claim 18, wherein the longitudinal shaft is coupled to the first yoke by a first axial joint. 21. The implant of claim 1 1, wherein the socket replacement linkage comprises a second hinge attachment portion and a socket bone attachment portion. 22. The implant of claim 21, wherein the second hinge attachment portion is coupled to the socket bone attachment portion by a second axial joint. 23. The implant of claim 11, wherein the socket replacement linkage comprises a socket mounting plate coupled to a second yoke, the second yoke coupled to the second hinge connector. 24. The implant of claim 23, wherein the socket mounting plate is dome shaped to mate with a natural socket formation or a surgically prepared socket formation of the socket bone. 25. The implant of claim 23, wherein the socket mounting plate is coupled to the second yoke by a second axial joint. 26. The implant of claim 23, wherein the second yoke maintains a fixed angled position relative to a central axis of the socket mounting plate. 27. The implant of claim 26, wherein the fixed angled position is adjustable. 28. The implant of claim 23, wherein the second yoke is coupled to the socket mounting plate at a radial distance from a central axis of the socket mounting plate. 29. The implant of claim 28, wherein the radial distance is adjustable. 30. The implant of claim 1 1, wherein the universal joint is covered by a boot. 31. The implant of claim 1 1 , wherein the offset measured as the shortest distance between the rotation axes of the first and second hinge connectors is greater than 9mm. 32. The implant of claim 18, wherein the offset measured as the shortest distance between the rotation axes of the first and second hinge connectors is greater than the yoke width of the first yoke. 33. The implant of claim 23, wherein the offset measured as the shortest distance between the rotation axes of the first and second hinge connectors is greater than the yoke width of the second yoke. 34. The implant of claim 1 1, wherein the anatomical ball and socket joint is a shoulder joint, the socket bone is a scapula providing a glenoid socket, and the long bone is a humerus 35. The implant of claim 34, wherein the socket replacement linkage is adapted to be attached to the glenoid socket and the ball replacement linkage is adapted to be attached to the humerus. 36. The implant of claim 1 1, wherein the anatomical ball and socket joint is a hip joint, the socket bone is a pelvis providing an acetabulum socket, and the long bone is a femur. 37. The implant of claim 36, wherein the socket replacement linkage is adapted to be attached to the acetabulum socket and the ball replacement linkage is adapted to be attached to the femur. 38. The implant of claim 1 1, adapted to be installed in the anatomical ball and socket joint in a revision surgery. 39. A method of replacing an anatomical ball and socket joint with a linked orthopedic implant, the method comprising: preparing a socket bone and a long bone of the ball and socket joint; and attaching the linked orthopedic implant of any one of claims 1 to 38 to the socket bone and the long bone. 40. The method of claim 39, wherein a socket attachment linkage of the linked orthopedic implant is attached at a tilt angle relative to a central axis of a socket formed in the socket bone. 41. The method of claim 39, wherein a socket attachment linkage of the linked orthopedic implant is attached at a distance relative to a central axis of a socket formed in the socket bone. 42. The method of claim 39, wherein the linked orthopedic implant is assembled in situ.
